heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 03 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: This period, starting 26 November 2025, is ranked 4th among the longest rainy periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 10 days so far.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 6 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (200°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.6°C), inside temperature (down 2.6°C), and outside temperature (down 2.2°C) and more moisture-laden rainfall (up 0.7 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in feels like temperature (up 6.2°C), rainfall (down 0.4 mm), outside temperature (up 4.7°C), and dew point temperature (up 4.4°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 29.1m higher than yesterday, 30.9m lower than the past week, 93.4m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 553m minimum and 1064m maximum, with an average of 825.1m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 856.0m and 918.5m.

summary.summary: Inside; relatively cool weather. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, low humidity will be noticeable and light winds will prevail. Periods of rain anticipated, the sky may alternate between sunny and overcast, and dim or diffuse light conditions. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.

washing.phrase: The humidity is too high (95%) for your washing to dry quickly.  Any drying will take longer than usual.
